Why is Maharashtra's Rs 100 Will registration move getting so much attention?Anyone who owns assets such as a flat, land, jewellery, fixed deposits, shares or mutual funds should consider making a Will. Even small savings can become the reason for family disagreements if there is no clear-written instruction from the owner.

Live: AIADMK to exit NDA? Buzz grows over tie-up with DMK to form governmentTamil Nadu Government Formation News Live Updates: The post-poll deadlock in Tamil Nadu has intensified, with Vijay's Tamilaga Vettri Kazhagam reportedly threatening a mass resignation of its 107 MLAs if either the DMK or the AIADMK attempts to form the government. The move signals growing anxiety within Vijay's camp as negotiations over government formation remain stalled despite TVK emerging as the single largest party with 108 seats in the 234-member Assembly. With the support of the Congress and its five MLAs, TVK still falls short of the majority mark of 118, making smaller parties and independents crucial to the final outcome. The political uncertainty deepened after reports of backchannel communication between the DMK and AIADMK to prevent TVK from assuming power. Congress' decision to back TVK has also strained opposition unity, prompting the DMK to declare that the INDIA bloc has effectively collapsed in Tamil Nadu. Meanwhile, eyes are also on the AIADMK, who won 47 seats, with many of its MLAs camping in Puducherry resorts amid political uncertainty. Stay tuned to IndiaToday.in for the latest updates.

From dragon fruit farming to CM reckoning: Dilip Ghosh's Bengal comeback storyLeading Bengal CM face Dilip Ghosh slammed the Trinamool Congress, alleging that Mamata Banerjee turned "netas into goondas and goondas into netas," and said the BJP may later admit "useful" leaders. He claimed her regime is coming to an end, accused her of undermining democratic institutions, and said the party's priority would be restoring law and order.

Live: UAE air defence systems intercept missiles and dronesUS-Israel-Iran War Live Updates: Diplomatic momentum and military pressure are moving in parallel as the US and Iran edge closer to a potential agreement to end the war, even as key differences remain unresolved. Both sides are discussing a short memorandum that could halt hostilities and open a 30-day window to address nuclear limits, sanctions relief and security in the Strait of Hormuz, though Tehran has said it is still reviewing the proposal and will respond through Pakistani mediators. US President Donald Trump has struck a mixed tone, saying the situation is under control and that negotiators want to make a deal very much. He warned that bombing could resume if Iran rejects the terms. He has also indicated that any deal could involve Iran transferring enriched uranium to the US and halting activity at underground facilities. On the ground, tensions remain high. The US military said it disabled an Iranian-flagged tanker in the Gulf of Oman as enforcement actions around the Strait continue, even as Iranian authorities signalled that passage could proceed under new procedures. Regional dynamics are also shifting, with Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu holding consultations with US officials amid concerns over possible last-minute concessions in the talks. Israel has simultaneously stepped up military operations, with Netanyahu confirming a strike in Beirut targeting a senior Hezbollah commander and warning that "every enemy and murderer" would be pursued. Fresh strikes have also been reported in Lebanon and Gaza. Stay tuned with indiatoday.in for all live updates.

Will hit Iran harder, more violently if there's no peace deal: Trump on Hormuz clashUS President Donald Trump claimed three American destroyers were attacked while crossing the Strait of Hormuz, warning Iran that the US would hit back "a lot harder" if it failed to sign a deal quickly.
